2026-04-17T10:41:53.8160479Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8161036Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8161467Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8161827Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8162142Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8162384Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8162544Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8162728Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8162897Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T10:41:53.8163129Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T10:41:53.8163884Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. 2026-04-17T10:45:17.3405417Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T10:42:59.487509122Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58285,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Update Core and Responses READMEs for new logging and session-ID features (beta.22 / beta.2)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary)\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs:\\n\\n1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n\\n2. **Responses beta.2** — Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation via SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` (63-char lowercase hex); structured `Information`-level logs added to all endpoint handlers; new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage call.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` is a single sentence that says \\u0026#34;Enable ASP.NET Core logging to diagnose startup issues\\u0026#34; — it does not mention the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, what it logs, at which levels, or that query strings are excluded.\\n- The `Key concepts` section of the Core README has no entry for the inbound request logging behaviour.\\n- The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` does not document the new handler-level structured logs, the `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, or how to surface them.\\n- The Responses README has no explanation of the `agent_session_id` field or how its value is determined (deterministic hash vs. random fallback).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~82) and replace it with an expanded description:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\n`AgentHostBuilder` automatically enables structured HTTP request logging via `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. For every inbound request the middleware emits structured log entries under the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` category:\\n\\n| Event | Level | Fields |\\n|-------|-------|--------|\\n| Request started | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `xRequestId`, `clientRequestId`, `traceId` |\\n| Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |\\n| Request completed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Security note:** Query strings are never logged to avoid capturing sensitive data.\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Information\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources.\\n```\\n\\n**Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Inbound request logging\\n\\n`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Log category: `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Internal.ResponseEndpointHandler`.\\n\\nKey log events per endpoint:\\n\\n- **POST /responses (create):** response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, streaming flag, background flag, store flag, model.\\n- **POST /responses (non-streaming completion):** final status and output item count.\\n- **GET /responses/{id}:** response ID, retrieved status, output item count.\\n- **POST /responses/{id}/cancel:** response ID and result status.\\n- **DELETE /responses/{id}:** response ID and success flag.\\n- **GET /responses/{id}/input_items:** response ID.\\n\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n\\nEnable these logs in `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Information\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n```\\n\\n**Also add** a `### Session ID` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Thread safety` entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session ID (`agent_session_id`)\\n\\nEach response carries an `agent_session_id` that groups related responses into a conversation session. The value is resolved in priority order:\\n\\n1. Explicit value from the request payload.\\n2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable.\\n3. **Deterministic derivation** (default): SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is the conversation ID (preferred) or the partition key extracted from `previous_response_id`.\\n4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available.\\n\\nThis matches the cross-language session ID contract so sessions are consistent whether the client uses the .NET or Python SDK.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation update — the changes are explanatory prose and tables. Existing snippets do not need to be modified.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nBoth READMEs already contain all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The edits above only expand existing sections and add sub-sections within `Key concepts`.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms no broken snippet placeholders.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24560713579/agentic_workflow) · ● 598.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58285\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-17T10:45:17.3471521Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T10:42:59.704729695Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58285,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Update Core and Responses READMEs for new logging and session-ID features (beta.22 / beta.2)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary)\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs:\\n\\n1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n\\n2. **Responses beta.2** — Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation via SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` (63-char lowercase hex); structured `Information`-level logs added to all endpoint handlers; new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage call.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` is a single sentence that says \\u0026#34;Enable ASP.NET Core logging to diagnose startup issues\\u0026#34; — it does not mention the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, what it logs, at which levels, or that query strings are excluded.\\n- The `Key concepts` section of the Core README has no entry for the inbound request logging behaviour.\\n- The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` does not document the new handler-level structured logs, the `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, or how to surface them.\\n- The Responses README has no explanation of the `agent_session_id` field or how its value is determined (deterministic hash vs. random fallback).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~82) and replace it with an expanded description:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\n`AgentHostBuilder` automatically enables structured HTTP request logging via `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. For every inbound request the middleware emits structured log entries under the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` category:\\n\\n| Event | Level | Fields |\\n|-------|-------|--------|\\n| Request started | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `xRequestId`, `clientRequestId`, `traceId` |\\n| Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |\\n| Request completed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Security note:** Query strings are never logged to avoid capturing sensitive data.\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Information\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources.\\n```\\n\\n**Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Inbound request logging\\n\\n`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Log category: `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Internal.ResponseEndpointHandler`.\\n\\nKey log events per endpoint:\\n\\n- **POST /responses (create):** response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, streaming flag, background flag, store flag, model.\\n- **POST /responses (non-streaming completion):** final status and output item count.\\n- **GET /responses/{id}:** response ID, retrieved status, output item count.\\n- **POST /responses/{id}/cancel:** response ID and result status.\\n- **DELETE /responses/{id}:** response ID and success flag.\\n- **GET /responses/{id}/input_items:** response ID.\\n\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n\\nEnable these logs in `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Information\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n```\\n\\n**Also add** a `### Session ID` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Thread safety` entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session ID (`agent_session_id`)\\n\\nEach response carries an `agent_session_id` that groups related responses into a conversation session. The value is resolved in priority order:\\n\\n1. Explicit value from the request payload.\\n2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable.\\n3. **Deterministic derivation** (default): SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is the conversation ID (preferred) or the partition key extracted from `previous_response_id`.\\n4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available.\\n\\nThis matches the cross-language session ID contract so sessions are consistent whether the client uses the .NET or Python SDK.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation update — the changes are explanatory prose and tables. Existing snippets do not need to be modified.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nBoth READMEs already contain all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The edits above only expand existing sections and add sub-sections within `Key concepts`.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms no broken snippet placeholders.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24560713579/agentic_workflow) · ● 598.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58285\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-17T10:45:17.3600255Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T10:43:25.531356443Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4280857957,\"number\":58268,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2:\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation.\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n- **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars.\\n- **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400.\\n- **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only \\\"The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\\" It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn't mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn't mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs.\\n- No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n#### 1a. Add a \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection under Key concepts\\n\\nAfter the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nThe `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is\\ncreated with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and\\nInputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found`\\n(indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible.\\n\\nWhen no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section\\n\\nReplace the existing bullet list with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains\\n  ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any\\n  supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id`\\n  request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters).\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state\\n  (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an\\n  `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `\\\"Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.\\\"`.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was\\n  created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not\\n  match the key provided in this request.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has\\n  already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section\\n\\nReplace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage call logging\\n\\nWhen the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`),\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and\\n`Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and latency\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`,\\n  `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry)\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"Logging\\\": {\\n    \\\"LogLevel\\\": {\\n      \\\"Azure.AI.AgentServer\\\": \\\"Information\\\"\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are\\nexplanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is\\ndesired, add a new test file:\\n\\n**Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs`\\n\\nWrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion`\\nand place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README's new \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all\\nrequired sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no\\nsections need to be created from scratch.\\n\\nCurrent structure (all present):\\n1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection added\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u003e Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24552776867/agentic_workflow) · ● 371.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026type=issues)\\n\\n\\u003c!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: Update Docs, engine: copilot, model: auto, id: 24552776867, workflow_id: update-samples-and-docs, run: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24552776867 --\\u003e\\n\\n\\u003c!-- gh-aw-workflow-id: update-samples-and-docs --\\u003e\\n\\u003c!-- gh-aw-workflow-call-id: Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/update-samples-and-docs --\\u003e\",\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,
